items.title: A Specialist Blocked At Home Is A Specialist Recruited Abroad — MMA
items.summary: A specialist blocked at home is a specialist recruited abroad. And the patient left behind is in a public hospital. The post A Specialist Blocked At Home Is A Specialist Recruited Abroad — MMA appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Growing Old Without Growing Poor
items.summary: If Malaysia chooses to act now — on retirement income, social security, health care and family support — we can ensure that our citizens grow old without growing poor. The post Growing Old Without Growing Poor appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: When Constipation Becomes More Than Just Discomfort — Dr Tan Woei Ling, Dr Noor Hasliza Che Seman & Dr Marina Jokim Robert
items.summary: By increasing awareness, encouraging open conversations, and supporting holistic approaches to care, we can help improve not only bowel function, but also comfort, dignity, and overall quality of life. The post When Constipation Becomes More Than Just Discomfort — Dr Tan Woei Ling, Dr Noor Hasliza Che Seman & Dr Marina Jokim Robert appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Over 1,000 Aged Care Homes Operating Without Licence In Malaysia
items.summary: Malaysia may have more than 1,000 unlicensed aged care homes, raising concerns about oversight and safety standards. The warning comes as population ageing accelerates and fees for resort-like senior living reach RM8,000-RM9,000 a month. The post Over 1,000 Aged Care Homes Operating Without Licence In Malaysia appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Nadma Must Coordinate National Preparedness For El Niño — Susden Malaysia
items.summary: National preparedness for El Niño through 2027 is a shared national responsibility that requires the active participation of all levels of government and all segments of society. The post Nadma Must Coordinate National Preparedness For El Niño — Susden Malaysia appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Many Specialists From Master’s Programmes Rejected For Subspecialty Training Due To LNPT Requirement — Physician
items.summary: In the June 16 results of the latest subspecialty training intake, a significant number of specialists who completed local Master's programmes were rejected, mainly because they were short of one LNPT assessment. These rejections cut across various fields. The post Many Specialists From Master’s Programmes Rejected For Subspecialty Training Due To LNPT Requirement — Physician appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: The Ticking Time Bomb: Why Malaysia’s Health Crisis Is Also A Self-Inflicted Wound — Dr Rajeentheran Suntheralingam
items.summary: Health is not an entitlement that can be abused and subsequently repaired for a nominal fee at a government clinic. It is an individual and civic responsibility. The post The Ticking Time Bomb: Why Malaysia’s Health Crisis Is Also A Self-Inflicted Wound — Dr Rajeentheran Suntheralingam appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Why Healthy Ageing Must Be Malaysia’s Next Health Care Reform — Dr Darishini Gaanesamurthi
items.summary: As Malaysia transitions into an ageing nation, health care reform must move beyond treating disease to preserving function, independence, and quality of life throughout later life. The post Why Healthy Ageing Must Be Malaysia’s Next Health Care Reform — Dr Darishini Gaanesamurthi appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Incentivise States To Preserve Farmland For Food Production, Expert Tells Putrajaya
items.summary: Putrajaya should reward states that preserve farmland for food production, a UPM academic said, warning that the loss of paddy land to development could threaten Malaysia’s food security amid reliance on rice imports and climate-related risks to yields. The post Incentivise States To Preserve Farmland For Food Production, Expert Tells Putrajaya appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Deputy DG Touts Micro-Credentials For Non-Doctors Amid Shortage
items.summary: MOH deputy DG Dr Fariza calls for micro-credentialing and decentralised training for non-doctors to perform certain health care tasks, amid doctor shortages and long training pathways for doctors. This approach can improve access to care in remote areas. The post Deputy DG Touts Micro-Credentials For Non-Doctors Amid Shortage appeared first on CodeBlue .
